title: Optimized ERC721 mint with hitchhiked extraData, dual-error revert, and balance overflow check
explanation: or(ownershipPacked, to) writes the new owner into the lower 160 bits while preserving any extraData already in the upper 96 bits — this is the hitchhiking technique. Balance overflow and zero address check combined into one condition using mul(to, and(packed, MAX_BALANCE)): if to=0 the mul is 0, if balance overflows the AND is 0.
when_to_apply: ERC721 mint where token IDs are not necessarily sequential
when_not_to_apply: ERC721A style batch minting, tokens where extraData must be zero at mint time
